data.meta_description: Find wheelchair-accessible yacht charter in Marbella. Our 12.5m Azimut 39 offers step-free boarding for 11 guests. Book with confidence today.
data.summary: We offer wheelchair-accessible yacht charter in Marbella from Puerto Banús, with the Azimut 39 providing step-free boarding for up to 11 guests. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance.
key_facts: Azimut 39 (12.5m) offers step-free boarding for wheelchair users, max 11 guests.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's main marina.
Inclusions: licensed skipper, fuel, soft drinks, beer, wine, snacks, insurance, IVA.
Azimut 39 pricing: €749 for 2 hours, €2,299 for 8 hours (all inclusive).
Larger accessible vessels available on request via WhatsApp.
Marbella's calm Mediterranean waters are ideal for accessible cruising.
Puerto Banús has accessible parking and ramps near our berth.

faq.q: Which yachts in your fleet are wheelchair accessible?
faq.a: Our Azimut 39 (12.5m) is the most accessible yacht in our fleet, with a wide, step-free boarding platform and no raised thresholds between the main deck and saloon. It accommodates up to 11 guests. For larger groups, please contact us via WhatsApp to discuss other options.

faq.q: Can a wheelchair user board the Azimut 39 independently?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 features a low, wide gangway from the pontoon to the aft deck, allowing most manual wheelchair users to board with minimal assistance. Our skipper will be on hand to help. The yacht has level access to the main saloon and outdoor seating areas.

faq.q: Is there an accessible toilet on board?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 has a marine toilet (electric flush) located in the lower cabin. It is not fully wheelchair-accessible due to narrow doorways and a small step. We recommend contacting us to discuss specific needs before booking.

faq.q: What is the price for a wheelchair-accessible charter?
faq.a: The Azimut 39 is priced at €749 for 2 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, and €2,299 for 8 hours. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. No hidden costs.

faq.q: Do you provide any special equipment for disabled guests?
faq.a: We do not provide wheelchairs, hoists, or other medical equipment. Our standard charter includes a safe, stable boarding setup and assistance from the crew. Please bring any personal mobility aids you require.

faq.q: Is the marina at Puerto Banús accessible?
faq.a: Yes, Puerto Banús has accessible parking bays, ramps, and flat walkways. Our berth is close to the main entrance. Contact us before your charter for specific directions to the nearest accessible parking spot.

faq.q: Can I charter a larger accessible yacht for a group?
faq.a: Yes, we can arrange larger vessels like the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) or Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) on request. Please WhatsApp us with your requirements, and we will confirm accessibility and pricing.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: Your safety is our priority. If conditions are unsafe, we will offer a full refund or reschedule to another date. Marbella enjoys over 300 sunny days a year, so cancellations are rare.

data.body_html:
Finding a marbella yacht charter wheelchair accessible can be a challenge, but we make it straightforward. Our Azimut 39, a 12.5-metre motor yacht departing from Puerto Banús, offers step-free boarding and a spacious main deck for up to 11 guests. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance — so you can relax and enjoy the Costa del Sol from the water.

Why Choose the Azimut 39 for Wheelchair Accessibility?

The Azimut 39 is our go-to vessel for guests with limited mobility. Its wide, low boarding platform connects directly to the pontoon without steps. Once on board, the main saloon and outdoor seating areas are on one level, with no raised thresholds. The yacht is 12.5 metres long, giving plenty of room to manoeuvre a wheelchair. It departs from Puerto Banús, a marina with accessible parking and ramps.

Charter Options and Prices

We offer flexible durations for the Azimut 39:

  • 2 hours: €749
  • 4 hours: €1,299
  • 6 hours: €1,799
  • 8 hours: €2,299

All prices include skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No extra charges. For larger groups or alternative vessels (e.g., Mangusta 80, Ferretti 94), contact us via WhatsApp.

What to Expect on Your Accessible Charter

Your charter starts at Puerto Banús. Our skipper will meet you at the berth and assist with boarding. Once under way, you can cruise along the Marbella coastline, past the Golden Mile, La Concha mountain, and the beaches of Cabopino. We can anchor at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for a swim (if accessible for you). The Mediterranean is typically calm from May to October, with water temperatures reaching 25°C in August.

Accessibility of Puerto Banús Marina

Puerto Banús is one of the most accessible marinas in Spain. It has designated disabled parking bays near the port entrance, ramps at key points, and flat, wide walkways. Our berth is within 50 metres of the nearest accessible parking. The marina also has accessible toilets and restaurants. We recommend arriving 15 minutes early to park and board at a relaxed pace.

Booking Your Wheelchair-Accessible Yacht Charter

To book the Azimut 39, use the online booking system on our Boat Rental Marbella homepage. Select your date, duration, and number of guests. For any questions about accessibility, WhatsApp us before booking. We can confirm the specific boarding setup and discuss any additional needs. Payment is secure, and cancellations are free up to 48 hours before departure.

Frequently Asked Questions

Which yachts in your fleet are wheelchair accessible?

Our Azimut 39 (12.5m) is the most accessible yacht in our fleet, with a wide, step-free boarding platform and no raised thresholds between the main deck and saloon. It accommodates up to 11 guests. For larger groups, please contact us via WhatsApp to discuss other options.

Can a wheelchair user board the Azimut 39 independently?

The Azimut 39 features a low, wide gangway from the pontoon to the aft deck, allowing most manual wheelchair users to board with minimal assistance. Our skipper will be on hand to help. The yacht has level access to the main saloon and outdoor seating areas.

Is there an accessible toilet on board?

The Azimut 39 has a marine toilet (electric flush) located in the lower cabin. It is not fully wheelchair-accessible due to narrow doorways and a small step. We recommend contacting us to discuss specific needs before booking.

What is the price for a wheelchair-accessible charter?

The Azimut 39 is priced at €749 for 2 hours, €1,299 for 4 hours, and €2,299 for 8 hours. All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. No hidden costs.

Do you provide any special equipment for disabled guests?

We do not provide wheelchairs, hoists, or other medical equipment. Our standard charter includes a safe, stable boarding setup and assistance from the crew. Please bring any personal mobility aids you require.

Is the marina at Puerto Banús accessible?

Yes, Puerto Banús has accessible parking bays, ramps, and flat walkways. Our berth is close to the main entrance. Contact us before your charter for specific directions to the nearest accessible parking spot.

Can I charter a larger accessible yacht for a group?

Yes, we can arrange larger vessels like the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) or Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) on request. Please WhatsApp us with your requirements, and we will confirm accessibility and pricing.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Your safety is our priority. If conditions are unsafe, we will offer a full refund or reschedule to another date. Marbella enjoys over 300 sunny days a year, so cancellations are rare.
